behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT diarization/model output: speaker labels can be wrong/missing only when the real model processes multi-speaker audio. A stubbed transcript would hide this. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model-output property (speaker labels), not a multi-agentic-step correctness chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API and assert returned transcript segments include stable speaker labels that can be mapped to attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields; real infra/model output is not required to trigger parsing bugs. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on extraction logic itself, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parsing function with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and verify structured output includes exact assignee and task text pairs.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM extraction/coverage decisions from meeting content; deterministic stubs can always satisfy the condition and hide misses. Rejected Workflow because this checks one summary-quality outcome, not correctness across multiple agentic decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real meeting transcript with known attendee task assignments to the live summarizer and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'identified decisions' and tests routing into schema fields, which is deterministic mapping logic. Rejected Agentic because identification quality is out of scope here; routing can fail without any real model call.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the routing/mapping function with pre-identified decision objects and verify output populates `decisions` schema keys and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM output-quality and placement behavior (what gets extracted as decisions and where). Mocked outputs would mask real misclassification or omission. Rejected Workflow because the behavior asserts a single end output quality property rather than multi-step agentic handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts with known decision statements through the production summarizer and check those decisions appear in the `decisions` section instead of only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple sequential segment-level AI steps (transcribe/summarize per segment) plus merge coherence across steps. Single-step tests miss cross-step context loss/order errors. Rejected System because real infra alone is insufficient; the core failure is multi-agentic-step orchestration quality.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute recording end-to-end with production segmentation enabled, verify sequential segment processing occurred, and evaluate final merged summary for cross-segment coherence and preserved action items/decisions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans full pipeline with multiple real AI stages (STT then LLM summary) and end-to-end automation. Failures can emerge from cross-stage handoff/retry/state interactions that lower-level tests miss. Rejected Agentic because this is not one model decision boundary; it is an end-to-end multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production pipeline on a real meeting recording: upload, transcribe via live STT API, summarize via live LLM, and assert structured payload is delivered automatically with no manual correction step.
